name: Castelnuovo Grand Parade
description: Associazione Carneval de CastelnovoVia Milano 537014 Castelnuovo del Garda (VR)ItaliaTel: +39 349 7433259Web:http://www.carnevaldecastelnovo.it

name: San Francisco carnaval
description: Carnaval San Francisco will be held on Memorial Day weekend May 23 and 24, 2026. It features a two-day festival of dance, concerts, food and entertainment and the Grand Parade on Sunday, May 24. The parade features approximately 70 groups from many different cultures including Brazil, Trinidad, and Central and South America. There many bands performing at the festival.

name: Frog (Žabarski) Carnival, Ivanić-Grad
description: International Frog (Žabarski) Carnival in Ivanić-Grad Ivanić-Grad, the pride of Central Croatia, transforms into a vibrant carnival stage for the International Frog (Žabarski) Carnival — a unique celebration of tradition, imagination, rhythm and colour. As one of Croatia’s pioneering continental carnivals, the event brings together hundreds of participants and numerous carnival groups from Croatia and other European countries, including Slovenia, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Greece, Italy and beyond. Organized by the Carnival Association “Žaba” from Ivanić-Grad, in cooperation with the City of Ivanić-Grad and the local Tourist Board, the carnival features a lively parade through the town and group presentations at the Zelenjak Sports Park.

name: Helsinki Samba Carnaval
description: Helsinki Samba Carnaval is one of the most colorful and energetic summer events in Finland, bringing samba rhythms, dance performances, vibrant costumes, and carnival atmosphere to the streets of Helsinki. The event gathers samba schools, performers, and visitors from across Finland and abroad, celebrating cultural diversity, music, and community spirit. 🎭🥁 For more information:📧 fecc.finland@samba.fi

name: Vrnjačka Banja Carnival
description: Since the early 1960s, Vrnjačka Banja has carried the spirit of carnival tradition. Revived in 2005 by the Tourist Sports Center, the International Vrnjačka Banja Carnival has grown into one of the region’s most vibrant cultural events. Featuring parades, concerts, children’s programs, and international carnival groups, it attracts thousands of participants and visitors each year. Today, as a proud member of the Federation of European Carnival Cities (FECC), Vrnjačka Banja holds a prominent place on the world carnival map.

name: Pag summer carnival
description: The Pag Carnival is one of the most cherished traditions of the island’s community — a time for gathering, celebration, and preserving the cultural heritage that “Pažani” proudly uphold. Its history stretches back for centuries, even being mentioned in the Statute of Pag Municipality from 1433. Today, the town celebrates both a Winter Carnival (held since at least 1884 in its current form) and a Summer Carnival (the oldest in Croatia, dating from 1959).Central to the festivities are unique local customs such as Rićavac, Paška robinja, Paško kolo, traditional songs, the “funeral” and burning of Marko, folk costumes, and Paški tanac. Since 1998, the Carnival Association Markova kumpanija — a proud FECC member — has worked to safeguard and promote these traditions, engaging both locals and vi

name: Tikveshki Grozdober Kavadarci
description: In the heart of North Macedonia’s renowned wine region, Kavadarci bursts into festive life every early September during theTikveški Grozdober—a carnival that blends tradition, culture, and community flair. Throughout  years, the Tikveški Grozdober has maintained its essence—a triumphant kickoff to the grape harvest season, integrating folk dances, musical concerts, exhibitions, seminars, gastronomic delights, and lively carnival parades adorned with local flavor and pride.Contact Information 📍Location:Kavadarci, North Macedonia🌐Website:www.kavadarci.gov.mk📧Email:info@kavadarci.gov.mk

name: Strolls to the Mirror in Martigues, 19th edition
description: IN MARTIGUES (The Venice of Provence),Bouches-du-Rhône, France Presenting a collection of more than 200 Venice Carnival costumes, created and worn by more than110 costumed members of the Association “LesMasquésVenétiensde France” (The Venetian Masked Performers of France). For the 19th year, Martigues – known as “The Venice of Provence” – is transformed for a weekend to introduce you to the very symbol of the Art and Beauty of its big sister, “Venice, the Venetian.” 110magnificent costumedfigures from France and Europe will stroll for your enjoyment through the alleys and gardens, along the quays and squares. Martigues will live to the rhythm of charm and poetry

name: Civijaški Carnival
description: ČivijaškiCarnival inŠabacis an international event of satire, humour, and carnival spirit. It has been held since 2008 under the patronage of theCityofŠabacand organised by theŠabacTourism Organisation and the “ČivijaškiCarnival” Association. The carnival attracts participants and visitors from various countries, showcasing a vibrant array of floats, costumes, and performances that celebrate creativity and wit. Each year, the event promises a lively atmosphere filled with laughter, music, and the joyous spirit of community. This year, the carnival will be celebrated together with the traditionalČivijada, from September 18 to 20, 2025. Thanks to its reputation and continuity,Šabachosted the convention of the Federation of European Carnival Cities (FECC) in 2018, further solidifying its plac

name: Carnival of Vevcani
description: The Carnival of Vevcani, older than 1.400 years, is an interesting mix of paganism and modernism. It is held on 13 and 14 January (on the eve of and at the first day of the New Year, according to the old calendar). The main characteristics of the carnival are: archaism, secretiveness and improvisation. According to its specific features, it differs from all other carnivals in the world. The arena of the carnival events is the whole village of Vevcani, which turns in a boundaryless theatre of its kind during the carnival, where each house, each street is a scene onto which disguised people perform their plays like real actors. There are three traditional masks: bridegroom and bride, August the Stupid and musicians. Remaining masks are, in essence, major or minor carnival groups, who, throug

name: Kukeri Carnival “Starchevata”
description: If you want to feel the magic of the Bulgarian carnival tradition,if you wish to experience the spirit of the ancient local Kukeri tradition “Starchevata,”if you want to discover the color and energy of unique carnival groups from Europe and around the world – come to Razlog! Come to the city of traditions and experience the spirit of Razlog and Bulgaria! Nestled between the majestic Pirin, Rila, and Rhodope Mountains, Razlog has established itself as a modern tourist destination. Here you will find the largest golf course in the Balkans, the modern SPA resort of Banya with its mineral springs, as well as close proximity to Pirin National Park. Luxury hotels, cozy guest houses, and horse-riding centers offer a variety of opportunities for complete relaxation amidst the beauty of nature. Lo
organizer: Social Activities, Education, Culture, Sports and Tourism”

name: Carnival in Victoria, Gozo
description: TheCarnival in Victoria, Gozo(Malta) is one of the island’s most vibrant cultural traditions, celebrated every year in the days leading up to Lent. Festivities begin with anopening parade on the Saturday before the officialCarnival week, when the capital’s streets burst into life with music, dancers, and colourful costumes. The celebrations continue with lively parades along Republic Street with satirical floats and performances in Independence Square. Beyond Victoria,various villages in Gozo also organize their own Carnival spectacles, featuring dance performances, creative displays, and plenty of festive spirit, making the entire island come alive with joy and colour.
organizer: Cultural Heritage Directorate, Ministry for Gozo and Planning

name: Rijeka Carnival
description: The Rijeka Carnival, known as the city’s “fifth season,” is a centuries-old tradition held annually from January 17th (St. Anthony’s Day) until the start of Lent. In its modern form, it began in 1982, initiated by three masked groups – the Halubje Bell Ringers, Pehlin’s Party People, and the “No Problem” group – with the support of the Tourist Board. The program features a series of cultural, entertainment, and sporting events, culminating in the Children’s Carnival Parade and the International Carnival Parade. The Children’s Parade brings together thousands of youngsters in colorful costumes and allegorical floats, while the grand finale showcases lavish costumes, elaborate floats, and carnival groups from Rijeka, the surrounding area, and abroad. The Carnival is a symbol of Rijeka’s iden

name: Balinjerada Opatija
description: Opatija Carnival – A Tradition That Never Stops Inspiring! When winter grips the coast, Opatija answers with smiles, music, and masks – it’s carnival time! With decades of tradition, the Opatija Carnival transforms the city into a stage of imagination, satire, and shared joy for over a month. Two signature events steal the spotlight: Other highlights include the Masked Klapa Marathon, where vocal groups in costume perform popular songs with humour and carnival flair.

name: Losinj carnival
description: Lošinj Carnivalprimary goal is to preserve the island’s cultural heritage and pass it on to younger generations. We achieve this through a series of activities and events that have become a recognizable part of the local community’s offerings during the winter months, outside the tourist season. Through its efforts, the Association promotes the carnival tradition of the island of Lošinj in the media, throughout the Primorje-Gorski Kotar County, across Croatia, and even beyond the country’s borders. The Association begins its activities on the first Saturday after the feast of Saint Anthony the Abbot, known locally as “Antonja.” The carnival season ends with a grand parade on Shrove Tuesday, just before Ash Wednesday. Traditionally, the masqueraders take over the city of Mali Lošinj through

name: Pag winter carnival
description: PAG WINTER CARNIVAL– various events from the first Saturday after the Epiphany until Ash Wednesday: TANCI – masked balls with live music and guest appearances by famous show stars RIČAVCI – organized, singing visits of Pag’s carnivalists homes ZADNJI DANI – the last days – when all mentioned culminates during a three-day carnival-festival on the main city square,  starting with a masked parade on Carnival-Sunday and ending on Ash Wednesdaywith the burning of Marko – the culprit of all troubles.

name: The Arlon Carnival
description: The Arlon Carnival, held annually in the historic town of Arlon in Belgium, is a vibrant celebration rooted in local tradition and community spirit. Taking place in the period leading up to Lent, the carnival features colorful parades, lively music, traditional costumes, and festive gatherings. A key highlight is the participation of local groups and associations, who bring the streets to life with creative performances and floats. The Arlon Carnival reflects the rich cultural heritage of the region and offers an authentic carnival experience with a warm, welcoming atmosphere.

name: Diest Carnival
description: The Carnival of Diest, held annually in Belgium, is a traditional and community-driven celebration taking place in the pre-Lenten period. The event features vibrant parades with decorated floats, costumed groups, and lively music throughout the historic town center. Local carnival associations play a key role, creating a festive atmosphere with strong regional identity. The Diest Carnival combines long-standing traditions with a welcoming and family-friendly spirit.

name: Pétange Carnival
description: The Carnival of Pétange, held annually in Luxembourg, is a lively and family-oriented celebration taking place in the weeks before Lent. Known for its colorful parade, the event features decorated floats, costumed groups, music, and the traditional distribution of sweets to spectators. With strong participation from local associations, the carnival creates a festive and welcoming atmosphere, reflecting the community spirit and cultural traditions of the region.

name: Lugagnano carnival
description: The Carnival of Lugagnano has legendary origins: it is said that many years ago, a caravan of gypsies stopped in the Lugagnano plain so that women could give birth. They integrated with the locals and settled permanently. This legend is supported by the fact that in 1956 hundreds of gypsies stayed in Lugagnano for 15 days to celebrate the wedding of the daughter of the “Queen of the Gypsies of Europe.” In 1979, thanks to two friends from Lugagnano and the local parish priest, the first parade was held. In 1980, they created the carnival maskLo Tzigano, and since 1982 a “Tzigano” has been elected each year by the townspeople. Today, Lugagnano’s Carnival is considered one of the best and most popular in the Province of Verona. The next edition will take placeMarch 13–15, 2026: Friday, March
